title: USD Semantic Runtime Types Capability
purpose: Define cross-cutting runtime representation requirements for values that carry USD semantic identity, especially paths, field keys, tokens, and grammar-defined names. The contract prevents generated domain logic from using adapter strings or canonical dump text as the authoritative representation for data that should participate in path or token/name identity.

included: spec paths, prim paths, property paths, object paths, and variant-selection paths
path construction, path comparison, prefix checks, and path ordering
field names and known core metadata field keys
USD token scalar values and token arrays
grammar-defined names used as identity in layer, namespace, composition, or stage data
child prim names, property names, variant set names, variant names used in selected variant addressing, and schema/type names
conversion at parser, adapter, diagnostic, and serialization boundaries
deferred: requiring a particular C++ type, ABI layout, allocator, table, atom implementation, or global singleton
forcing user-authored display strings or arbitrary string-valued fields into token identity
changing the authored USD/document-model type of string-typed or specialized fields
asset identifier and resource location representation beyond not confusing them with object paths
deduplicating every string payload in generic unmodeled field values

model.SemanticPath: A target-native path abstraction such as a handle, interned path reference, indexed path node, or backend-native path object. It is not a raw string in domain storage.
model.SemanticName: A target-native identity abstraction for grammar-defined USD names used for lookup, equality, ordering, or path construction. It may share the token identity domain or use a target-native name atom.
model.FieldToken: A token or field atom used as the identity for known and extension field keys.
model.USDTokenValue: A target-native value for fields whose authored USD type is token or token[].
model.BoundaryString: A string accepted or emitted at parser, adapter, diagnostic, C ABI, or serialization boundaries. It is converted to or from the target-native semantic type before domain logic stores or compares it.
model.TypePreservingInterning: Optional interning or atomization of string-authored semantic names that does not change the authored field value type exposed by checked reads or canonical dumps.

laws: domain-facing APIs and long-lived runtime records must represent spec paths, object paths, prim paths, property paths, and variant-selection paths as SemanticPath values or equivalent target-native path references

a string cache for a path is allowed only as a derived diagnostic, display, adapter, or serialization cache; it must not be the only authoritative identity used by composition, stage population, or generated backend logic

path construction, path comparison, path hashing, path prefix checks, parent/name queries, and path ordering must consume the usd-paths capability or an equivalent target-native implementation that satisfies the path contract
field keys must be FieldToken values or equivalent field atoms; generated domain code must not key durable field maps by raw strings
USD token scalar and token-array field values must preserve token identity as USDTokenValue values or equivalent target-native token handles
grammar-defined names that participate in identity, lookup, ordering, or path construction must be stored as SemanticName values or equivalent target-native name handles in domain records

child prim names, property names, variant set names, selected variant names, and schema/type names must not be retained as raw strings in composition records, stage population indexes, or backend query state when they are used for identity decisions

interning a grammar-defined name must not change the authored field type: variantSetNames remains a listop<string> field, variantSelection remains its specialized map type, and displayName remains a string even if an implementation uses name handles internally

variant set names are identifier-like names; selected variant names follow the variant-name grammar and may contain characters not valid in identifiers, so any SemanticName implementation used for variants must support the variant-name domain and must not apply prim/property identifier restrictions to selected variant names

empty or missing semantic values may use an invalid/null handle when the surrounding contract permits absence, but that sentinel must remain distinguishable from a valid empty string or valid empty token when the value domain permits one
adapter JSON, textual USDA parsing, diagnostics, reports, and C ABI string outputs may expose strings, but those strings are boundary representations converted at the edge

asset identifiers and resolved resource locations are not USD object paths; they may use resource or asset abstractions and must not be coerced into path handles unless a field's specified type is ObjectPath or another path-valued type
composition provenance records that pair a source identifier with a spec path must keep the spec path as a SemanticPath value; composite strings such as source.usda:/Prim are boundary renderings only

generic field value containers may choose compact storage, std::variant-style storage, arenas, or backend-native values, but checked reads must preserve whether the stored USD/document-model type is path, token, string, specialized map, or another typed value
